title: [InstCombine] Fold `fcmp pred sqrt(X), 0.0 -> fcmp pred2 X, 0.0`

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on double precision examples. The patch pattern matching is type-agnostic and should optimize half/float precision sqrt comparisons equally.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @sqrt_fcmp_zero_half(half %arg0) {
#0:
  %sqrt = sqrt half %arg0
  %cmp = fcmp ule half %sqrt, 0x0000
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @sqrt_fcmp_zero_half(half %arg0) {
#0:
  %cmp = fcmp ule half %arg0, 0x0000
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
define i1 @sqrt_fcmp_zero_multiple_uses(double %arg0) {
  %sqrt = call double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %arg0)
  %cmp1 = fcmp one double %sqrt, 0.000000e+00
  %cmp2 = fcmp oeq double %sqrt, 0.000000e+00
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %and
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't address cases where sqrt result has multiple uses beyond the fcmp. The patch operates per-instruction and should optimize each fcmp independently regardless of use count.

alive2.src:
define i1 @sqrt_fcmp_zero_multiple_uses(double %arg0) {
  %sqrt = call double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %arg0)
  %cmp1 = fcmp one double %sqrt, 0.000000e+00
  %cmp2 = fcmp oeq double %sqrt, 0.000000e+00
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %and
}

  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %and
}

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples use double precision only; half precision (f16) is not explicitly covered though the pattern abstraction applies to all FP types

  ret i1 %cmp
}
